title: Provenance
description: Per-entity provenance metadata blending W3C PROV-O (process model: who-derived-from-what-when-by-which-activity) with Dublin Core Terms (record-level metadata: created, modified, source, license). Every canonical entity in islamicatlas-canonical MUST carry a provenance block. Provenance immutability principle: 'created' and the initial 'derived_from' set are write-once; subsequent modifications append to record_history and update 'modified', but never overwrite the original assertions.

source_id.description: Identifier of the upstream source. Either a CURIE-style identifier into a known source dataset ('yaqut:7842', 'bosworth-nid:42', 'le-strange:312', 'makdisi:muq-0024') or an iac PID once the source namespace is live ('iac:source-00000001').

source_type.description: Source category. 'primary_textual' = a primary source text (Yâkût Mu'cem itself). 'secondary_scholarly' = modern scholarly work (Bosworth NID, Le Strange Lands). 'tertiary_reference' = encyclopaedic (DİA, EI). 'manual_editorial' = an editorial assertion by the canonical store author. 'authority_file' = imported from an external authority (Wikidata cross-reference).

page_or_locator.description: Source-internal locator: page number ('cilt 2 sayfa 145'), folio reference, sectional pointer ('NID #42, p.94'), or a structured locator. Free-text by design to accommodate the heterogeneity of pre-modern citation conventions.

extraction_method.description: How the data was extracted from the source.

edition_or_version.description: Specific edition / version of the source (e.g., 'Wüstenfeld 1866-1873', 'Bosworth 2004 EUP repr.', 'OpenITI 2023.1.1').

derived_from.description: List of upstream sources from which the canonical entity is derived. PROV-O: prov:wasDerivedFrom. At least one source is required.

pipeline_name.description: Name of the ETL pipeline that produced this canonical record. Examples: 'canonicalize_dynasty', 'canonicalize_place_yaqut', 'merge_place_multisource'.

pipeline_version.description: Semver of the pipeline at the time of generation. Required for reproducibility.

commit_sha.description: Git commit SHA of the canonical-store repository at generation time. Optional but strongly recommended for reproducibility.

input_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the concatenated input source files used to produce this entity, computed by the pipeline. Enables detecting silent source mutations.

generated_by.description: PROV-O: prov:wasGeneratedBy. The activity that produced the canonical record.

generated_at.description: PROV-O: prov:generatedAtTime. ISO 8601 datetime when the canonical record was generated.

attributed_to.description: PROV-O: prov:wasAttributedTo. URI (preferably ORCID) of the person responsible for the canonical record. Defaults to the canonical-store maintainer's ORCID.

created.description: Dublin Core: dcterms:created. Datetime the canonical record was first instantiated. Write-once.

modified.description: Dublin Core: dcterms:modified. Datetime of the most recent modification to the canonical record. May equal 'created' if never modified.

license.description: License URI under which the canonical record is published. Defaults to CC-BY-SA 4.0 (the canonical-store data licence).

change_type.description: Kind of change. 'merge' = two canonical entities consolidated into this one (the others are deprecated). 'split' = this entity was created by separating from another. 'deprecate' = soft-delete (the entity is no longer authoritative but the PID is kept stable for citation continuity). 'revive' = un-deprecation.

release.description: Release tag at which the change took effect.

merged_from.description: When change_type='merge', the PIDs of the entities subsumed into this one (now deprecated).

record_history.description: Audit log of changes to this canonical record across releases. Strictly append-only; entries are never deleted or rewritten.

deprecated.description: Soft-delete flag. Once an entity is created, its PID lives forever for citation stability. If the entity is later judged invalid (e.g., merged into another, or sourced from a since-retracted dataset), 'deprecated' is set to true and a record_history entry is appended; the PID continues to resolve but downstream consumers know to follow the redirect.

deprecated_in_favor_of.description: When deprecated=true and the entity has been merged into another, the canonical PID that supersedes this one. Public URI resolver issues a 301 redirect to the successor.

$comment: If 'deprecated'=true then 'deprecated_in_favor_of' SHOULD be present (semantic-level rule, enforced by pipeline; not by JSON Schema in v0.1.0 to keep schema simple).
